channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: EUR/USD Falls for 3rd Day as Geopolitics and Strong US Dollar Dictate Terms
item.description: The EUR/USD fell for a third day due to geopolitics and a strong US dollar. The currency pair has been volatile in recent weeks, with investors seeking safe-haven assets amid global uncertainty.

item.title: Reported Ship Seizures Add to Anxiety in Oil Markets
item.description: Oil prices hovered above $100 a barrel as reported ship seizures added to market anxiety. The White House said President Trump does not view Iran's reported seizures as a cease-fire violation.

item.title: Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in U.S.-hosted talks with Israel
item.description: Lebanon will seek a ceasefire extension in US-hosted talks with Israel. The current ceasefire has yielded a significant reduction in violence, but attacks continue in southern Lebanon. The U. S.-mediated ceasefire has yielded a significant reduction in violence, but attacks have continued in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops have seized a self-declared buffer zone.

item.title: US orders citizens to flee Lebanon as fragile ceasefire ends, airport roads blocked
item.description: The US ordered citizens to flee Lebanon as a fragile ceasefire ended and airport roads were blocked. The move came amid ongoing violence in the region. US orders citizens to flee Lebanon as fragile ceasefire ends, airport roads blocked.

item.title: After U.S. Aid Fell by 99%, E.U. Pledges $106 Billion Loan to Ukraine
item.description: The E. U. pledged a $106 billion loan to Ukraine after US aid fell by 99%. The loan is heavily weighted toward military spending, reflecting a view that the war is far from over.

item.title: Rodrigo Duterte to face ICC trial after judges confirm charges
item.description: Rodrigo Duterte will face ICC trial after judges confirmed charges against him. Pre-trial judges “unanimously confirmed all the charges against Rodrigo Roa Duterte and committed him to trial,” the ICC said in a statement.

item.title: Navy Secretary Axed. And $17 Billion Trump-Class Warship?
item.description: US Navy Secretary John Phelan was sacked amid disputes over Trump-class warships. US Navy Secretary John Phelan was sacked Wednesday reoportedly amid disputes with Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth over the expensive new Trump-class warships; Hegseth reportedly favours mass-produced uncrewed ships to boost numbers.

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: The Revolutionary Guards gained power in Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death. The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: Will Bulgaria’s New Leader Cast His Lot With Europe or Russia?
item.description: Rumen Radev won elections in Bulgaria with a record of pro-Russia statements. With a record of pro-Russia statements, Rumen Radev handily won elections this week. Now Bulgarians and his E. U.

item.title: Iran Shows Off Its Ballistic Missile At Tehran Rally Amid US Ceasefire
item.description: Iran showcased its Qadr ballistic missile at a Tehran rally amid a US ceasefire. The Qadr is an upgraded version of the older Shahab-3A missile. Iran Shows Off Its Ballistic Missile At Tehran Rally Amid US Ceasefire.

item.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
item.description: Americans are moving to Vietnam and Thailand for lower costs and stress. Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

item.title: UN leadership hopefuls stress need for peace and restoring confidence
item.description: UN leadership hopefuls stressed the need for peace and restoring confidence. UN leadership hopefuls stress need for peace and restoring confidence France 24.

item.title: India's state elections test Modi alliance as Tamil Nadu, West Bengal voting begins
item.description: Voting began in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal to test Modi's alliance. The contests are closely watched as a gauge of public sentiment towards the ruling coalition led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

item.title: Candidates vying for U.N. secretary-general role face questions
item.description: Four candidates are vying to replace UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres this year. Who will replace U. N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U. N.

item.title: Putin's approval rating falls amid state-imposed internet restrictions
item.description: Putin's approval rating is falling due to Russia's internet restrictions. In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions. and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.

item.title: After being held up for months, Ukraine set to get $100B loan from EU
item.description: Ukraine will receive a $100 billion loan from the European Union after months of delay. Taking advantage of the electoral defeat of Hungary's Viktor Orban, European Union countries have moved to approve a $100 billion loan to Ukraine.

item.title: Iran seizes container ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
item.description: Iran seized container ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran seizes container ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire CBC.

item.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
item.description: US Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his post. The Pentagon said it is effective immediately. US Navy Secretary John Phelan is the latest high-ranking military leader to leave the administration in recent months.

item.title: Indigenous land defenders are being killed, AI is scraping their knowledge
item.description: Indigenous land defenders are being killed and their knowledge is being scraped by AI. Indigenous land defenders are being killed, AI is scraping their knowledge grist. org.

item.title: FairPrice widens price freeze to over 300 essentials until May 31
item.description: FairPrice Group froze prices on over 300 essentials until May 31. FairPrice Group first froze prices on 100 everyday essentials earlier this month. FairPrice widens price freeze to over 300 essentials until May 31.
